habitat: Evergreen forest; dipterocarp forest; swamp forest; heath forest; forest edge; secondary forest; and plantations
story: Few birds capture the imagination quite like the Black And Yellow Broadbill (Eurylaimus ochromalus). As a member of the Eurylaimidae family, it has carved out a unique niche in Evergreen forest; dipterocarp forest; swamp forest; heath forest; forest edge; secondary forest; and plantations. Its conservation status is Near Threatened (NT), a classification that drives crucial research and protection efforts. The Black And Yellow Broadbill's presence is a vital sign of its ecosystem's health, making its preservation a priority. This poster is a tribute to the Black And Yellow Broadbill, celebrating its role in the natural world and inspiring a deeper appreciation for biodiversity.
Every poster is crafted to inspire curiosity about our planet's incredible biodiversity.
